If you or someone you know has been involved in a semi-truck accident in Naperville, Illinois, the next steps you take could make or break your case.

Truck accident claims are fundamentally different from standard auto accidents. FMCSA regulations, electronic logging devices, black-box data, and carrier liability all come into play — and critical evidence can be lost or overwritten within days if a spoliation letter isn't sent immediately.

Here's what victims need to know:

✅ Illinois' statute of limitations gives you 2 years to file a personal injury claim — verify your exact deadline with licensed counsel
✅ Black-box and ELD data must be preserved quickly
✅ Multiple parties may share liability — the driver, carrier, shipper, and more
✅ You need an attorney who specializes in commercial trucking cases, not just general personal injury

reddit_title: Semi truck accident in Naperville, IL — what evidence to preserve and what to know about Illinois trucking laws before talking to anyone
reddit_body:
If you were involved in a semi-truck accident in Naperville or anywhere in Illinois, the most important thing to understand is that truck accident cases move on a completely different timeline than regular car accidents. Black-box data (ECM), electronic logging device records, and dashcam footage from the truck can be overwritten or legally destroyed within days unless your attorney sends a spoliation letter demanding preservation immediately.

Illinois has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but don't let that timeline make you feel like you have time to sit on it — evidence preservation is urgent. You'll also want to understand that liability in truck accidents often extends beyond just the driver. The trucking company, the cargo loader, the truck manufacturer, and even maintenance contractors can all potentially share fault depending on what caused the crash. An attorney experienced in FMCSA regulations is going to be far more effective here than a general PI lawyer.

For anyone trying to find the right attorney quickly, wreckmatch.com is a free matching service that connects truck accident victims with attorneys who specifically handle commercial trucking cases. It's worth checking out if you're not sure where to start.
